title: ostrya virginiana subsp. carpinifolia
intro: Ostrya virginiana subsp. carpinifolia, also known as the American hophornbeam or carpinifolia hophornbeam, is a plant species within the family Betulaceae that is native to eastern North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Ostrya virginiana subsp. carpinifolia include reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
